Title: The microRNA ppe-miR393 mediates auxin-induced peach fruit softening by promoting ethylene production

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fenglu; Xiacui; Dongtao; Hujingmilu; Huayu; Youmingbaitao; Qinwang
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid, the direct precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene production and peach fruit softening; strongly induced by auxin/NAA and by PpIAA13
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpACS1 was identified as the key ethylene biosynthetic gene associated with the difference between melting flesh and stony hard peach. 2. PpIAA13 directly binds to and activates the PpACS1 promoter, increasing ethylene emission and softening. 3. PpACS1 expression is induced by NAA and by ppe-miR393a overexpression, and reduced when PpIAA13 is silenced or miR393 is suppressed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during ripening and NAA treatment; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; transient overexpression/silencing in peach fruit; ethylene and firmness measurements
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low at S1-S3 and strongly induced at S4 in melting flesh peach; low in stony hard peach; elevated after NAA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PpACS1 promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC for dual-luciferase; 2,000-bp native promoter in pAbAi for Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; committed-step enzyme; major determinant of ethylene production difference between melting flesh and stony hard peach
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; 1 mM NAA postharvest treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, accession PRJNA576753; RNA-seq of NAA-treated 'XC' fruit at 4 d, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher PpACS1 activity is associated with faster softening and reduced shelf life
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target to modulate postharvest softening and shelf life; lowering expression could reduce rapid softening, whereas activation could accelerate ripening when desired
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states PpACS1 is more strongly auxin responsive than PpACO1 and is the key player in ethylene production differences between SH and MF peaches

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fenglu; Xiacui; Dongtao; Hujingmilu; Huayu; Youmingbaitao; Qinwang
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Cleaves and represses PpTIR1, indirectly increasing PpIAA13 and activating PpACS1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Auxin-induced upstream switch linking auxin perception to ethylene biosynthesis and softening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PpACS1 expression, ethylene production, and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ppe-miR393a is expressed at higher levels in melting flesh than stony hard peach and is induced by NAA. 2. It directly cleaves PpTIR1 mRNA. 3. Overexpression increases ethylene and softening, whereas suppression reduces them and the effect can be restored by NAA.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: small RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; RLM-5' RACE; GUS transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient overexpression and STTM suppression in peach fruit; ethylene and firmness assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low during S1-S3 and highly induced at S4 in melting flesh peach; low in stony hard peach; induced after NAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for MIR393a overexpression and STTM393a expression in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Endogenous IAA; exogenous NAA; fruit ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely auxin-induced feedforward loop promoting ethylene biosynthesis via repression of auxin receptor transcript
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression accelerates softening and may shorten shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for delaying softening by repression or target mimicry; tuning expression may extend shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1 mM NAA postharvest treatment; ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains auxin-responsive cis-elements; acts together with ppe-miR393b

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fenglu; Xiacui; Hujingmilu; Huayu; Youmingbaitao; Qinwang
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Cleaves and represses PpTIR1, indirectly elevating PpIAA13 and PpACS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cooperates with ppe-miR393a as an auxin-responsive upstream regulator of ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene production and fruit softening indirectly through the PpTIR1-PpIAA13-PpACS1 module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ppe-miR393b is more abundant in melting flesh than stony hard peach and is induced early by NAA. 2. It cleaves PpTIR1 similarly to ppe-miR393a. 3. It likely cooperates with ppe-miR393a in NAA-triggered softening.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: small RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; RLM-5' RACE; GUS transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low during S1-S3 and highly induced at S4 in melting flesh peach; low in stony hard peach; induced 2-6 d after NAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for MIR393b and STTM393b in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Endogenous IAA; exogenous NAA; fruit ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely auxin-induced feedforward loop promoting ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May accelerate postharvest softening and shorten shelf life when elevated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for fruit shelf-life engineering through miRNA tuning or target mimicry
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1 mM NAA postharvest treatment; ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: May compensate temporally for ppe-miR393a during early NAA-triggered softening

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fenglu; Xiacui; Dongtao; Hujingmilu; Huayu; Youmingbaitao; Qinwang
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TIR1/AFB F-box auxin receptor; SCF complex component; leucine-rich repeat protein
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling input controlling ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein interaction and likely auxin-dependent promotion of Aux/IAA turnover; negatively affects PpIAA13/PpACS1 module in this context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Interacts with PpIAA13 and negatively affects accumulation of PpIAA13, thereby reducing PpACS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as the direct miR393 target and key relay linking auxin-responsive miRNA to ethylene output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses ethylene production and maintains firmer fruit when abundant; lower PpTIR1 is associated with higher ethylene and softer fruit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpTIR1 is a validated target of ppe-miR393a/b. 2. It physically interacts with PpIAA13 in an IAA-dependent manner. 3. Lower PpTIR1 correlates with higher PpIAA13 and PpACS1, whereas transient overexpression reduces ethylene and increases firmness.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: bioinformatic prediction; RLM-5' RACE; GUS transient cleavage assay; yeast two-hybrid; BIFC; expression profiling; transient overexpression in peach fruit
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Opposite to miR393; lower at S4 in melting flesh than stony hard peach; reduced after NAA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S:PpTIR1-GUS in Nicotiana assay; transient overexpression construct in peach fruit
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: SCF TIR1 auxin receptor complex inferred; direct interaction with PpIAA13 validated
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of auxin-responsive module subject to miRNA-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing PpTIR1 may accelerate softening; increasing PpTIR1 may delay ripening and soften less
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for delaying rapid peach softening by overexpression or editing miR393 target site, with caution about broader auxin-related effects
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA addition in Y2H/BIFC; 1 mM NAA postharvest treatment; ripening stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpAFB2 was also predicted as a miR393 target but did not interact with PpIAA13 and was not supported as the functional mediator of this softening module

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fenglu; Xiacui; Dongtao; Hujingmilu; Huayu; Youmingbaitao; Qinwang
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aux/IAA family with conserved Domains I-IV; Domain III with βαα-fold linked to DNA recognition
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation of biosynthetic gene promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds to the PpACS1 promoter TGTG…TGTG element and activates transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator that directly converts auxin signaling status into higher PpACS1 expression and ethylene output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene production and fruit softening by activating PpACS1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested auxin signaling genes, PpIAA13 significantly trans-activates the PpACS1 promoter, whereas tested PpARFs do not. 2. PpIAA13 directly binds the PpACS1 promoter as shown by Y1H and EMSA. 3. Overexpression increases PpACS1, ethylene emission, and softening, while silencing decreases them and NAA restores the phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing; ethylene and firmness assays; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low at S1-S3 and strongly induced at S4 in melting flesh peach; low in stony hard peach; induced by NAA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S or pGreenII overexpression construct for PpIAA13; native PpACS1 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: PpTIR1 status; endogenous IAA; exogenous NAA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with PpTIR1; no protein complex beyond this direct pair was experimentally established
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced expression accelerates softening and may reduce shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point to control postharvest texture by reducing PpIAA13 activity or expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts unusually as a direct DNA-binding activator of PpACS1, rather than only indirectly via ARFs
